2	name: Cortex frontend must render in Safari 15 WKWebView
3	description: Hard Tauri-on-macOS-12 constraint — no oklch, no color-mix, no Tailwind v4 color utilities, no regex lookbehind, inline styles for spacing
4	type: feedback
5	originSessionId: c3e7ed21-61bb-49c2-80b8-39f2703c7c99
6	---
7	Cortex is a Tauri 2.x desktop app. On macOS 12 the Tauri webview is Safari 15's WKWebView, which breaks silently on modern CSS + JS features. This is not a polish concern — unsupported features cause blank pages and unreadable UI.
8	
9	**Do not use in any Cortex frontend code:**
10	- `oklch()` colors — not supported
11	- `color-mix()` — not supported
12	- **Tailwind v4 color utility classes** (`bg-red-500`, `text-blue-400`, etc.) — they emit `oklch()` internally and break silently
13	- Regex lookbehind `(?<=...)` or `(?<!...)` — not supported in Safari 15 regex engine
14	
15	**Do use:**
16	- Inline `style={{ padding: 16, marginBottom: 20 }}` for spacing
17	- CSS variables with **hex** values only (`var(--accent)` where `--accent: #3b82f6`)
18	- Lucide icons (pure SVG, always safe)
19	- Tailwind layout utilities that don't emit colors: `flex`, `flex-col`, `min-w-0`, `min-h-0`, `overflow-*`, `animate-spin`, `gap-*`, etc. are fine
20	- Hex colors when you need a specific shade inline: `color: "#ef4444"` not `color: "red-500"`
21	
22	**Why:** Discovered during the first UI polish pass of Cortex — whole pages went blank until the oklch issue was identified. Documented in `test-vault/patterns/Pattern - Safari 15 Compatibility.md`.
23	
24	**How to apply:** Before writing any new frontend component for Cortex, default to the inline-style + CSS-var + hex idiom already used in `src/components/sidebar/SessionsPanel.tsx`, `PlansPanel.tsx`, `LiveSessionView.tsx`, `PlanRunnerView.tsx`. Mirror that pattern exactly. If in doubt, `grep -rn oklch src/` should stay empty.
25

1	---
2	name: Tauri v2 gotchas that bite Cortex-style desktop apps
3	description: PATH + spawn permissions + blocked window.prompt + Vite watcher ignore list — four Tauri-specific pitfalls all from real Phase B breakage
4	type: feedback
5	originSessionId: c3e7ed21-61bb-49c2-80b8-39f2703c7c99
6	---
7	Four Tauri v2 gotchas. Each caused a real Phase B bug in commit `69cacb0` that I missed in initial review. All four fixes are in the Cortex codebase as reference implementations.
8	
9	## 1. GUI-launched apps have a barebones PATH
10	
11	macOS Finder/Dock launches (and Tauri dev webview) start processes with no shell exports. `/opt/homebrew/bin` (ARM), `/usr/local/bin` (Intel), `$HOME/.local/bin`, and anything from `.zshrc`/`.bashrc` are all missing.
12	
13	**When it hits:** Any `tauri_plugin_shell::Command::spawn()` on a binary that lives outside `/usr/bin` or `/bin`. Rust `Command::new("claude")` does a PATH lookup, finds nothing, spawn fails.
14	
15	**The fix (in `src-tauri/src/run/execute.rs`):**
16	```rust
17	let current_path = std::env::var("PATH").unwrap_or_default();
18	let extended_path = format!("/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:{}", current_path);
19	let (mut rx, child) = shell
20	    .command("claude")
21	    .args(&args)
22	    .current_dir(&cwd)
23	    .env("PATH", &extended_path)   // ← always
24	    .spawn()?;
25	```
26	
27	**Why:** Same root cause as Cortex's Phase A `dotenvy::from_path(".env")` at startup — GUI env is barebones, you hydrate it explicitly.
28	
29	**How to apply:** Any time you spawn an external binary from a Tauri command, extend PATH at the spawn site or (better) at Tauri app startup. Never assume `claude`, `git`, `bun`, `node`, or `rg` will be on PATH just because they are in your shell.
30	
31	## 2. `.spawn()` and `.execute()` need SEPARATE scoped permissions
32	
33	`tauri-plugin-shell` 2.x splits these: `shell:allow-execute` permits the one-shot `.execute()` API, `shell:allow-spawn` permits the streaming `.spawn()` API. They have their own scope allowlists. Both must be declared in `capabilities/*.json` if your app uses both.
34	
35	**Cortex example (`src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`):**
36	```json
37	{
38	  "identifier": "shell:allow-execute",
39	  "allow": [{"name": "claude", "cmd": "claude", "args": true, "sidecar": false}]
40	},
41	{
42	  "identifier": "shell:allow-spawn",
43	  "allow": [{"name": "claude", "cmd": "claude", "args": true, "sidecar": false}]
44	}
45	```
46	
47	Plus `"shell:allow-kill"` and `"shell:allow-stdin-write"` as needed.
48	
49	**How to apply:** When wiring any shell spawn feature in Tauri, grep your capabilities JSON for both `allow-execute` AND `allow-spawn` scope blocks. Missing the second silently returns "access denied" at spawn time.
50	
51	## 3. `window.prompt/alert/confirm` don't work in the Tauri webview
52	
53	macOS WKWebView blocks these legacy browser dialogs. They either return null immediately or no-op. Any code that relies on `window.prompt("...")` to capture user input WILL silently fail in Tauri even though it works in a regular browser.
54	
55	**Use instead:** inline React input fields that appear on button click with `autoFocus`, Enter-to-submit, Escape-to-cancel.
56	
57	**Cortex reference implementation:** `src/components/sidebar/PlansPanel.tsx` after commit `69cacb0` — the + New Plan and ✨ Draft Plan buttons both use this pattern. Copy it verbatim when building similar input flows.
58	
59	**How to apply:** Never use `window.prompt`, `window.confirm`, or `window.alert` in Cortex frontend code. Always a React state toggle + inline input + handler pattern.
60	
61	## 4. Vite HMR watcher must ignore vault-adjacent paths
62	
63	In `bun run tauri dev` mode, Vite's file watcher defaults to reloading on any file change under the project root. If your Tauri backend writes files inside a subdirectory (Cortex writes `.claude/settings.json` on every `open_vault`, the KG writes `.cortex/kg.json` after extraction), that write triggers Vite HMR → browser reload → re-runs `open_vault` → re-writes settings → infinite reload loop.
64	
65	**Two fixes needed (both in Cortex):**
66	
67	**a) Content-equality guard in the writer** (`src-tauri/src/commands/vault.rs`):
68	```rust
69	if let Ok(existing) = std::fs::read_to_string(&settings_path) {
70	    if existing == pretty { return Ok(()); }
71	}
72	```
73	
74	**b) Vite watcher ignore list** (`vite.config.ts`):
75	```ts
76	server: {
77	  watch: {
78	    ignored: ['**/test-vault/**', '**/.cortex/**', '**/.claude/**', /* ... */],
79	  }
80	}
81	```
82	
83	Both are needed: the content-equality guard prevents unnecessary writes in the steady state; the watcher ignore list prevents HMR from ever observing vault writes even when they do happen (KG save after extraction, session notes after Phase B runs).
84	
85	**How to apply:** Any Tauri + Vite app where the Rust backend writes files inside the watched tree MUST exclude those paths from the Vite watcher. Default pattern to copy into `vite.config.ts`:
86	```ts
87	ignored: ['**/vault/**', '**/test-vault/**', '**/.cortex/**', '**/.claude/**', '**/node_modules/**', '**/.git/**']
88	```
89	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

---
name: Tauri v2 gotchas that bite Cortex-style desktop apps
description: PATH + spawn permissions + blocked window.prompt + Vite watcher ignore list — four Tauri-specific pitfalls all from real Phase B breakage
type: feedback
originSessionId: c3e7ed21-61bb-49c2-80b8-39f2703c7c99
---
Four Tauri v2 gotchas. Each caused a real Phase B bug in commit `69cacb0` that I missed in initial review. All four fixes are in the Cortex codebase as reference implementations.

## 1. GUI-launched apps have a barebones PATH

macOS Finder/Dock launches (and Tauri dev webview) start processes with no shell exports. `/opt/homebrew/bin` (ARM), `/usr/local/bin` (Intel), `$HOME/.local/bin`, and anything from `.zshrc`/`.bashrc` are all missing.

**When it hits:** Any `tauri_plugin_shell::Command::spawn()` on a binary that lives outside `/usr/bin` or `/bin`. Rust `Command::new("claude")` does a PATH lookup, finds nothing, spawn fails.

**The fix (in `src-tauri/src/run/execute.rs`):**
```rust
let current_path = std::env::var("PATH").unwrap_or_default();
let extended_path = format!("/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:{}", current_path);
let (mut rx, child) = shell
    .command("claude")
    .args(&args)
    .current_dir(&cwd)
    .env("PATH", &extended_path)   // ← always
    .spawn()?;
```

**Why:** Same root cause as Cortex's Phase A `dotenvy::from_path(".env")` at startup — GUI env is barebones, you hydrate it explicitly.

**How to apply:** Any time you spawn an external binary from a Tauri command, extend PATH at the spawn site or (better) at Tauri app startup. Never assume `claude`, `git`, `bun`, `node`, or `rg` will be on PATH just because they are in your shell.

## 2. `.spawn()` and `.execute()` need SEPARATE scoped permissions

`tauri-plugin-shell` 2.x splits these: `shell:allow-execute` permits the one-shot `.execute()` API, `shell:allow-spawn` permits the streaming `.spawn()` API. They have their own scope allowlists. Both must be declared in `capabilities/*.json` if your app uses both.

**Cortex example (`src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`):**
```json
{
  "identifier": "shell:allow-execute",
  "allow": [{"name": "claude", "cmd": "claude", "args": true, "sidecar": false}]
},
{
  "identifier": "shell:allow-spawn",
  "allow": [{"name": "claude", "cmd": "claude", "args": true, "sidecar": false}]
}
```

Plus `"shell:allow-kill"` and `"shell:allow-stdin-write"` as needed.

**How to apply:** When wiring any shell spawn feature in Tauri, grep your capabilities JSON for both `allow-execute` AND `allow-spawn` scope blocks. Missing the second silently returns "access denied" at spawn time.

## 3. `window.prompt/alert/confirm` don't work in the Tauri webview

macOS WKWebView blocks these legacy browser dialogs. They either return null immediately or no-op. Any code that relies on `window.prompt("...")` to capture user input WILL silently fail in Tauri even though it works in a regular browser.

**Use instead:** inline React input fields that appear on button click with `autoFocus`, Enter-to-submit, Escape-to-cancel.

**Cortex reference implementation:** `src/components/sidebar/PlansPanel.tsx` after commit `69cacb0` — the + New Plan and ✨ Draft Plan buttons both use this pattern. Copy it verbatim when building similar input flows.

**How to apply:** Never use `window.prompt`, `window.confirm`, or `window.alert` in Cortex frontend code. Always a React state toggle + inline input + handler pattern.

## 4. Vite HMR watcher must ignore vault-adjacent paths

In `bun run tauri dev` mode, Vite's file watcher defaults to reloading on any file change under the project root. If your Tauri backend writes files inside a subdirectory (Cortex writes `.claude/settings.json` on every `open_vault`, the KG writes `.cortex/kg.json` after extraction), that write triggers Vite HMR → browser reload → re-runs `open_vault` → re-writes settings → infinite reload loop.

**Two fixes needed (both in Cortex):**

**a) Content-equality guard in the writer** (`src-tauri/src/commands/vault.rs`):
```rust
if let Ok(existing) = std::fs::read_to_string(&settings_path) {
    if existing == pretty { return Ok(()); }
}
```

**b) Vite watcher ignore list** (`vite.config.ts`):
```ts
server: {
  watch: {
    ignored: ['**/test-vault/**', '**/.cortex/**', '**/.claude/**', /* ... */],
  }
}
```

Both are needed: the content-equality guard prevents unnecessary writes in the steady state; the watcher ignore list prevents HMR from ever observing vault writes even when they do happen (KG save after extraction, session notes after Phase B runs).

**How to apply:** Any Tauri + Vite app where the Rust backend writes files inside the watched tree MUST exclude those paths from the Vite watcher. Default pattern to copy into `vite.config.ts`:
```ts
ignored: ['**/vault/**', '**/test-vault/**', '**/.cortex/**', '**/.claude/**', '**/node_modules/**', '**/.git/**']
```

3	description: --include-hook-events causes claude --print --output-format stream-json to exit immediately with 0 events under Tauri shell spawn
4	type: project

6	---
7	Phase B's `execute_plan` spawns `claude --print --output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages ...` with a long flag set. One flag, `--include-hook-events`, causes the spawned claude to exit immediately with 0 events emitted on stdout on every single run when invoked via Tauri's shell plugin.
8	
9	**Why:** Unknown. The leaked Claude Code source claims `--include-hook-events` requires only `--output-format stream-json` (which Phase B has) and the flag itself validates fine. But under Tauri spawn in GUI mode with a real Max plan auth path, the flag apparently makes claude exit before producing any output. A manual terminal invocation of the same command WITHOUT this flag produces a full 30+ event stream ending in a `result` event. Adding the flag back from the terminal also fails. Current Claude Code binary version: 2.1.97.
10	
11	**How to apply:** When modifying Cortex's `src-tauri/src/run/execute.rs::build_claude_args` or the draft_plan_from_goal spawn in `src-tauri/src/commands/plans.rs`, do NOT re-add `--include-hook-events` to the argv. If you need hook-related telemetry, find another path (inspect stream events directly, or call claude without this flag and handle any hook noise in the parser).
12	
13	**Why:** Fixed in commit `69cacb0` after the flag caused every Phase B run to silently fail with 0 events. Found during fresh-machine end-to-end testing that the user ran against my initial implementation.
14	
15	**Related to:** The leaked-source recon I did earlier in this project directory — the agent reported `--include-hook-events` worked under stream-json mode. Either the source lies, or the flag interacts badly with another flag Phase B sets. Not yet investigated further.

1	---
2	name: Cortex calls Claude on Vertex AI using future-labs convention
3	description: All Claude calls from Cortex must hit Vertex AI Anthropic, using the env-var + URL convention from future-labs registry.ts. No api.anthropic.com, no shell-out to claude.
4	type: project
5	originSessionId: f6f8d359-6bb8-4290-961c-3a7df46fb442
6	---
7	Cortex's Claude calls (cortex-extract Phase A, Phase B prepare_run, future LLM features) must go through **Google Cloud Vertex AI Anthropic**, mirroring the user's existing pattern at `~/Desktop/future-stuff/future-labs/lib/agent/provider/registry.ts`.
8	
9	**Why:** future-labs is the user's battle-tested Vertex setup. The same env vars, the same model ID format, the same endpoint. Reusing this convention means Cortex Just Works wherever the user already runs Vertex; diverging creates a second config surface to maintain.
10	
11	**How to apply:**
12	
13	1. **Env vars (read at first call, cache the manager):**
14	   - `GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON` (or `GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON`) — full SA JSON as a single env var string. Parse with `gcp_auth::CustomServiceAccount::from_json`.
15	   - `GCP_PROJECT_ID` (or `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT_ID`), fallback to `credentials.project_id`. Verified working project: `admachina-atomic-test-84`.
16	   - `GCP_REGION`, default `global` (load-balances across regions, preferred for resilience). `us-east5` also works. Both verified 2026-04-09.
17	   - **Load these from `~/Desktop/Cortex/.env` via `dotenvy` at Cortex startup**, NOT from the shell. R3b: GUI-launched `Cortex.app` from Finder gets a barebones environment, so file-based loading is mandatory. 7	Phase B (the plan-execution orchestrator) spawns Claude Code processes from Cortex. The auth strategy has TWO eras:
8	
9	**Phase B v1 (current):** spawn a normal Max-plan `claude` process. The user already has Claude Code on Max plan via OAuth/keychain — Phase B reuses that path, $0 cost.
10	
11	**How to dodge hook recursion:** the vault's `<vault>/.claude/settings.json` has Cortex hooks (Phase A). A normal spawn from inside that vault would re-fire those hooks → infinite loop. The fix is `--setting-sources user`: load user-level settings (`~/.claude/settings.json` — statusline, plugins, OAuth keychain) but skip project-level (`<vault>/.claude/settings.json` — the hooks). Pair with `--strict-mcp-config --mcp-config <per-run-config>` to enforce Cortex's MCP allowlist for the spawned run. Do NOT use `--bare` — `--bare` strictly disables OAuth/keychain reads, killing Max plan auth.
12	
13	**Why:** Max plan is unlimited for the user; Vertex would charge per token and require translating Cortex's `GCP_*` env vars into Claude Code's `ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_*` + `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` set. The Max plan path is simpler, cheaper, and the user's existing Claude Code workflow.
14	
15	**Future (custom TUI harness era):** Cortex builds its own claude-code-like TUI wrapper that runs arbitrary models through Vertex AI (Claude variants OR non-Claude models). At that point Vertex becomes the primary auth path for the orchestrator, and Phase B's spawn step gets replaced by direct model calls. Until that exists, do NOT preemptively wire Vertex into Phase B's spawn logic.
16	
17	**Three LLM contexts in Cortex (do not conflate):**
18	1. Interactive Claude Code (Max plan OAuth) — what the user runs directly. Calls Cortex's MCP server. Phase A's auto-capture path. Cortex itself does no LLM work here.
19	2. `cortex-extract` background job (Vertex AI via SA JSON) — Cortex's only standalone LLM call, used after a session ends to extract entities/relations into the KG. Vertex is required because no Claude Code session exists at that moment to delegate to.
20	3. Phase B-spawned Claude Code (Max plan OAuth via the `--setting-sources user` trick) — orchestrator-launched claude processes that execute `type: plan` notes. Inherits the user's Max plan auth from the parent env.
21	
22	**How to apply:** When designing or implementing Phase B's `execute_plan` Tauri command, assume Max plan OAuth as the default auth path. Spawn `claude --print --verbose --setting-sources user --strict-mcp-config --mcp-config <path>` (NOT `--bare`). Pass the parent process's env unchanged. Skip the Vertex env translation step entirely. The `phase-b-verified-state.md` v1 spike doc was written assuming Vertex; when the doc gets rewritten or read, treat the Max plan path as primary and Vertex as the future custom-harness fallback.

# Pattern: Safari 15 Compatibility

## Problem
Tauri on macOS 12 uses Safari 15's WKWebView. Modern CSS features break silently.

## What Breaks
| Feature | Status |
|---------|--------|
| `oklch()` colors | NOT supported |
| `color-mix()` | NOT supported |
| Tailwind v4 color classes | Output oklch internally |
| Regex lookbehind `(?<=...)` | NOT supported |

## Solution
Use inline `style={{}}` for all critical spacing. CSS variables with hex values only.

```tsx
// BAD — breaks in Safari 15
className="px-5 mb-4"

// GOOD — works everywhere
style={{ paddingLeft: 20, marginBottom: 16 }}
```

## Discovered During
UI refinement of [[Cortex App]]. First polish pass caused blank pages until we identified the issue.

#pattern #tauri #safari #css

# Cortex Project Instructions

Cortex is a Tauri 2.x desktop app (Rust + React + TypeScript) that provides knowledge graph-powered note-taking with AI-assisted planning. This file encodes hard-won constraints that future Claude Code sessions must follow.

## Project Structure

```
Cortex/
├── src-tauri/           # Rust backend (Tauri commands, MCP server, KG extraction)
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── commands/    # Tauri commands (vault.rs, plans.rs, sessions.rs, etc.)
│   │   ├── run/         # Phase B plan execution (execute.rs)
│   │   ├── mcp/         # MCP server implementation
│   │   └── lib.rs
│   └── capabilities/    # Tauri security permissions
├── src/                 # React frontend
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── sidebar/     # SessionsPanel, PlansPanel, LiveSessionView, etc.
│   │   └── ...
│   └── ...
├── cortex-extract/      # Standalone KG extraction binary (calls Claude on Vertex AI)
├── test-vault/          # Test notes (DO NOT use as examples for normal vault content)
│   ├── patterns/        # Project patterns documentation
│   └── sessions/        # Auto-captured session notes
└── vite.config.ts       # Frontend build config
```

---

## CRITICAL: Safari 15 Compatibility (macOS 12 WKWebView)

Tauri on macOS 12 uses Safari 15's WKWebView. Modern CSS features cause **blank pages**.

### DO NOT USE in any frontend code:
- `oklch()` colors — NOT supported
- `color-mix()` — NOT supported  
- **Tailwind v4 color utility classes** (`bg-red-500`, `text-blue-400`, etc.) — emit `oklch()` internally
- Regex lookbehind `(?<=...)` or `(?<!...)` — NOT supported

### DO USE:
- Inline `style={{ padding: 16, marginBottom: 20 }}` for spacing
- CSS variables with **hex values only**: `var(--accent)` where `--accent: #3b82f6`
- Lucide icons (pure SVG, always safe)
- Tailwind layout utilities: `flex`, `flex-col`, `min-w-0`, `overflow-*`, `gap-*` (anything non-color)
- Hex colors for specific shades: `color: "#ef4444"` NOT `color: "red-500"`

**Verify:** `grep -rn oklch src/` must stay empty.

**Reference implementations:**
- `src/components/sidebar/SessionsPanel.tsx`
- `src/components/sidebar/PlansPanel.tsx`
- `src/components/sidebar/LiveSessionView.tsx`

**Why:** Discovered during UI polish — whole pages went blank. Documented in `test-vault/patterns/Pattern - Safari 15 Compatibility.md`.

---

## Tauri v2 Gotchas

### 1. GUI-Launched Apps Have Barebones PATH

macOS Finder/Dock launches don't inherit shell PATH. `/opt/homebrew/bin`, `/usr/local/bin`, `$HOME/.local/bin` are missing.

**Fix (required for all spawns):**
```rust
let current_path = std::env::var("PATH").unwrap_or_default();

WKWebView blocks legacy browser dialogs. They return null or no-op.

# Cortex Project Instructions

Cortex is a Tauri 2.x desktop app (Rust + React + TypeScript) that provides knowledge graph-powered note-taking with AI-assisted planning. This file encodes hard-won constraints that future Claude Code sessions must follow.

## Project Structure

```
Cortex/
├── src-tauri/           # Rust backend (Tauri commands, MCP server, KG extraction)
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── commands/    # Tauri commands (vault.rs, plans.rs, sessions.rs, etc.)
│   │   ├── run/         # Phase B plan execution (execute.rs)
│   │   ├── mcp/         # MCP server implementation
│   │   └── lib.rs
│   └── capabilities/    # Tauri security permissions
├── src/                 # React frontend
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── sidebar/     # SessionsPanel, PlansPanel, LiveSessionView, etc.
│   │   └── ...
│   └── ...
├── cortex-extract/      # Standalone KG extraction binary (calls Claude on Vertex AI)
├── test-vault/          # Test notes (DO NOT use as examples for normal vault content)
│   ├── patterns/        # Project patterns documentation
│   └── sessions/        # Auto-captured session notes
└── vite.config.ts       # Frontend build config
```

---

## CRITICAL: Safari 15 Compatibility (macOS 12 WKWebView)

Tauri on macOS 12 uses Safari 15's WKWebView. Modern CSS features cause **blank pages**.

### DO NOT USE in any frontend code:
- `oklch()` colors — NOT supported
- `color-mix()` — NOT supported  
- **Tailwind v4 color utility classes** (`bg-red-500`, `text-blue-400`, etc.) — emit `oklch()` internally
- Regex lookbehind `(?<=...)` or `(?<!...)` — NOT supported

### DO USE:
- Inline `style={{ padding: 16, marginBottom: 20 }}` for spacing
- CSS variables with **hex values only**: `var(--accent)` where `--accent: #3b82f6`
- Lucide icons (pure SVG, always safe)
- Tailwind layout utilities: `flex`, `flex-col`, `min-w-0`, `overflow-*`, `gap-*` (anything non-color)
- Hex colors for specific shades: `color: "#ef4444"` NOT `color: "red-500"`

**Verify:** `grep -rn oklch src/` must stay empty.

**Reference implementations:**
- `src/components/sidebar/SessionsPanel.tsx`
- `src/components/sidebar/PlansPanel.tsx`
- `src/components/sidebar/LiveSessionView.tsx`

**Why:** Discovered during UI polish — whole pages went blank. Documented in `test-vault/patterns/Pattern - Safari 15 Compatibility.md`.

---

## Tauri v2 Gotchas

### 1. GUI-Launched Apps Have Barebones PATH

macOS Finder/Dock launches don't inherit shell PATH. `/opt/homebrew/bin`, `/usr/local/bin`, `$HOME/.local/bin` are missing.

**Fix (required for all spawns):**
```rust
let current_path = std::env::var("PATH").unwrap_or_default();
let extended_path = format!("/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:{}", current_path);
shell.command("claude")
    .env("PATH", &extended_path)  // Always extend PATH
    .spawn()?;
```

**Reference:** `src-tauri/src/run/execute.rs`

### 2. Shell Spawn Requires TWO Permission Types

`tauri-plugin-shell` 2.x needs BOTH:
- `shell:allow-execute` — for `.execute()` 
- `shell:allow-spawn` — for `.spawn()`

Plus `shell:allow-kill` and `shell:allow-stdin-write` as needed.

**Reference:** `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`

### 3. window.prompt/alert/confirm Don't Work

WKWebView blocks legacy browser dialogs. They return null or no-op.

**Use instead:** React state + inline input fields with `autoFocus`, Enter-to-submit, Escape-to-cancel.

**Reference:** `src/components/sidebar/PlansPanel.tsx` (+ New Plan and ✨ Draft Plan buttons)

### 4. Vite HMR Must Ignore Vault Paths

Backend writes to `.claude/settings.json`, `.cortex/kg.json` trigger infinite reload loops.

**Required in `vite.config.ts`:**
```ts
server: {
  watch: {
    ignored: [
      '**/test-vault/**',
      '**/.cortex/**', 
      '**/.claude/**',
      '**/vault/**',
      '**/node_modules/**',
      '**/.git/**'
    ],
  }
}
```

**Plus content-equality guard in writers:** Check if existing file content matches before writing.

---

## Phase B (Plan Execution) Rules

### Auth Strategy

Phase B spawns **Max-plan Claude Code sessions** via `--setting-sources user`, NOT Vertex AI.

**Correct spawn command:**
```bash
claude --print --verbose \
  --setting-sources user \
  --strict-mcp-config --mcp-config <path> \
  # ... other flags
```

**Why:** User has Max plan (unlimited). `--setting-sources user` loads user-level settings (`~/.claude/settings.json` with OAuth) but skips project-level hooks (`<vault>/.claude/settings.json`) to avoid recursion.

**Do NOT use:**
- `--bare` — kills OAuth/Max plan auth
- Vertex env translation — unnecessary for spawned claude processes

### DO NOT Use --include-hook-events Flag

This flag causes `claude --print --output-format stream-json` to exit immediately with 0 events under Tauri shell spawn.

**Why:** Unknown root cause. Flag validates fine but breaks every spawn. Fixed in commit `69cacb0`.

**Where to check:** `src-tauri/src/run/execute.rs::build_claude_args`

---

## Cortex's Three LLM Contexts (Do Not Conflate)

1. **Interactive Claude Code (Max plan OAuth)** — user runs directly, calls Cortex MCP server
2. **`cortex-extract` background job (Vertex AI)** — standalone KG extraction after session ends
3. **Phase B spawned Claude Code (Max plan OAuth)** — orchestrator launches plan execution

### Vertex AI Auth (cortex-extract ONLY)

**Env vars (load from `~/Desktop/Cortex/.env` via `dotenvy` at startup):**
- `GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON` or `GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON`
- `GCP_PROJECT_ID` or `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT_ID` (verified: `admachina-atomic-test-84`)
- `GCP_REGION` (default: `global`, verified: `global` and `us-east5`)

**Endpoint:**
```
POST https://{region}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{region}/publishers/anthropic/models/{model_id}:rawPredict
```
(If `region == "global"`, drop the `{region}-` prefix)

**Body:** Standard Anthropic Messages API, but:
- **Omit** `model` field (it's in URL)
- **Add** `"anthropic_version": "vertex-2023-10-16"`

**Auth:** `gcp_auth::AuthenticationManager::get_token()`, pass as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

**Model IDs (verified working 2026-04-09):**
- `claude-haiku-4-5@20251001` — fast, use for cortex-extract
- `claude-sonnet-4-5@20250929` — balanced
- `claude-opus-4-6` — powerful

**Structured output:** Vertex Anthropic does NOT support native structured output. Use forced `tool_use` with `tool_choice: {type: "tool", name: "..."}`.

**Do NOT:**
- Shell out to `claude --bare -p` for Cortex LLM calls
- Call `api.anthropic.com` — user's Anthropic account has zero credit balance

**Reference implementation:** `~/Desktop/future-stuff/future-labs/lib/agent/provider/registry.ts`

---

## UI Design Rules

### DO NOT:
- Use Playwright screenshot loops to evaluate design
- Tweak padding values and call it "design"
- Make incremental changes without understanding what's structurally wrong

### DO:
- Go component-by-component: read, understand, rewrite properly
- Study real apps (Obsidian, Linear, VS Code) before writing CSS
- Add proper depth (shadows, layering, visual hierarchy)
- Ensure text never touches container edges (proper inner padding)

**Recurring issues to fix:**
- Text crammed against edges (headers, buttons)
- No visual depth or hierarchy
- Section titles wrong size
- Sidebar header text at left edge
- Buttons with no inner margin

---

## test-vault/ Notes

The `test-vault/` directory contains:
- **patterns/** — Project pattern documentation (valid examples)
- **sessions/** — Auto-captured Claude Code sessions (auto-generated, often messy)
- Various test notes

**Do NOT use test-vault session notes as examples of good vault content.** They're auto-captured during development and may contain debugging artifacts, incomplete context, or test data.

**DO use test-vault/patterns/ notes** — these are curated patterns like:
- `Pattern - Safari 15 Compatibility.md`
- `Pattern - Parallel Agent Teams.md`  
- `Pattern - Self-Bootstrapping.md`

---

## Development Workflow

### Build Commands
```bash
# Frontend only
bun run dev

# Full Tauri dev (Rust + frontend)
bun run tauri dev

# Build release
bun run tauri build

# Rust checks
cargo check --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml
cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml
```

### Gate Checks Between Changes
Always run between major changes:
```bash
cargo check --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml
bun run build  # Verify frontend compiles
```

---

## MCP Server

Cortex runs its own MCP server for knowledge graph operations. Tools include:
- `cortex/search` — semantic search
- `cortex/get-note` — retrieve note by path
- `cortex/entity-profile` — get entity from KG
- `cortex/query-graph` — graph queries
- And more...

Phase B-spawned Claude processes get a restricted MCP config via `--strict-mcp-config` to control which servers they can access.

---

## File Paths Quick Reference

### Frontend Components
- Sidebar panels: `src/components/sidebar/`
- Main views: `src/components/`

### Backend Commands
- Vault ops: `src-tauri/src/commands/vault.rs`
- Plan execution: `src-tauri/src/run/execute.rs`
- Plan commands: `src-tauri/src/commands/plans.rs`
- Session capture: `src-tauri/src/commands/sessions.rs`

### Configuration
- Tauri permissions: `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`
- Vite config: `vite.config.ts`
- Environment: `~/Desktop/Cortex/.env` (loaded via `dotenvy`)

### Documentation
- This file: `CLAUDE.md`
- Project patterns: `test-vault/patterns/Pattern - *.md`
- Auto-captured sessions: `test-vault/sessions/` (noisy, use with caution)

---

## When In Doubt

1. Check existing implementations in the same category
2. Read the relevant `test-vault/patterns/` note if one exists  
3. Verify Safari 15 compat before writing frontend code
4. Run `cargo check` + `bun run build` before claiming "it works"
5. Never bypass Tauri security (don't add blanket shell permissions)

# Cortex Project Instructions

Cortex is a Tauri 2.x desktop app (Rust + React + TypeScript) that provides knowledge graph-powered note-taking with AI-assisted planning. This file encodes hard-won constraints that future Claude Code sessions must follow.

## Project Structure

- `src-tauri/` — Rust backend (Tauri commands, MCP server, KG extraction)
- `src/components/` — React frontend components
- `cortex-extract/` — Standalone KG extraction binary (Vertex AI)
- `test-vault/` — Test notes (patterns/ = docs, sessions/ = auto-generated)
- Key files: `vite.config.ts`, `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`

---

## CRITICAL: Safari 15 Compatibility (macOS 12 WKWebView)

Tauri on macOS 12 uses Safari 15's WKWebView. Modern CSS features cause **blank pages**.

### DO NOT USE in any frontend code:
- `oklch()` colors — NOT supported
- `color-mix()` — NOT supported  
- **Tailwind v4 color utility classes** (`bg-red-500`, `text-blue-400`, etc.) — emit `oklch()` internally
- Regex lookbehind `(?<=...)` or `(?<!...)` — NOT supported

### DO USE:
- Inline `style={{ padding: 16, marginBottom: 20 }}` for spacing
- CSS variables with **hex values only**: `var(--accent)` where `--accent: #3b82f6`
- Lucide icons (pure SVG, always safe)
- Tailwind layout utilities: `flex`, `flex-col`, `min-w-0`, `overflow-*`, `gap-*` (anything non-color)
- Hex colors for specific shades: `color: "#ef4444"` NOT `color: "red-500"`

**Verify:** `grep -rn oklch src/` must stay empty.

**Reference implementations:**
- `src/components/sidebar/SessionsPanel.tsx`
- `src/components/sidebar/PlansPanel.tsx`
- `src/components/sidebar/LiveSessionView.tsx`

    .env("PATH", &extended_path)  // Always extend PATH

```

`tauri-plugin-shell` 2.x needs BOTH:
- `shell:allow-execute` — for `.execute()` 
- `shell:allow-spawn` — for `.spawn()`

**Reference:** `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`

WKWebView blocks legacy browser dialogs. They return null or no-op.

      '**/vault/**',
      '**/node_modules/**',

}

# Cortex Project Instructions

Cortex is a Tauri 2.x desktop app (Rust + React + TypeScript) that provides knowledge graph-powered note-taking with AI-assisted planning. This file encodes hard-won constraints that future Claude Code sessions must follow.

## Project Structure

- `src-tauri/` — Rust backend (Tauri commands, MCP server, KG extraction)
- `src/components/` — React frontend components
- `cortex-extract/` — Standalone KG extraction binary (Vertex AI)
- `test-vault/` — Test notes (patterns/ = docs, sessions/ = auto-generated)
- Key files: `vite.config.ts`, `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`

---

## CRITICAL: Safari 15 Compatibility (macOS 12 WKWebView)

Tauri on macOS 12 uses Safari 15's WKWebView. Modern CSS features cause **blank pages**.

### DO NOT USE in any frontend code:
- `oklch()` colors — NOT supported
- `color-mix()` — NOT supported  
- **Tailwind v4 color utility classes** (`bg-red-500`, `text-blue-400`, etc.) — emit `oklch()` internally
- Regex lookbehind `(?<=...)` or `(?<!...)` — NOT supported

### DO USE:
- Inline `style={{ padding: 16, marginBottom: 20 }}` for spacing
- CSS variables with **hex values only**: `var(--accent)` where `--accent: #3b82f6`
- Lucide icons (pure SVG, always safe)
- Tailwind layout utilities: `flex`, `flex-col`, `min-w-0`, `overflow-*`, `gap-*` (anything non-color)
- Hex colors for specific shades: `color: "#ef4444"` NOT `color: "red-500"`

**Verify:** `grep -rn oklch src/` must stay empty.

**Reference implementations:**
- `src/components/sidebar/SessionsPanel.tsx`
- `src/components/sidebar/PlansPanel.tsx`
- `src/components/sidebar/LiveSessionView.tsx`

**Why:** Discovered during UI polish — whole pages went blank. Documented in `test-vault/patterns/Pattern - Safari 15 Compatibility.md`.

---

## Tauri v2 Gotchas

### 1. GUI-Launched Apps Have Barebones PATH

macOS Finder/Dock launches don't inherit shell PATH. `/opt/homebrew/bin`, `/usr/local/bin`, `$HOME/.local/bin` are missing.

**Fix (required for all spawns):**
```rust
let current_path = std::env::var("PATH").unwrap_or_default();
let extended_path = format!("/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:{}", current_path);
shell.command("claude")
    .env("PATH", &extended_path)  // Always extend PATH
    .spawn()?;
```

**Reference:** `src-tauri/src/run/execute.rs`

### 2. Shell Spawn Requires TWO Permission Types

`tauri-plugin-shell` 2.x needs BOTH:
- `shell:allow-execute` — for `.execute()` 
- `shell:allow-spawn` — for `.spawn()`

Plus `shell:allow-kill` and `shell:allow-stdin-write` as needed.

**Reference:** `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`

### 3. window.prompt/alert/confirm Don't Work

WKWebView blocks legacy browser dialogs. They return null or no-op.

**Use instead:** React state + inline input fields with `autoFocus`, Enter-to-submit, Escape-to-cancel.

**Reference:** `src/components/sidebar/PlansPanel.tsx` (+ New Plan and ✨ Draft Plan buttons)

### 4. Vite HMR Must Ignore Vault Paths

Backend writes to `.claude/settings.json`, `.cortex/kg.json` trigger infinite reload loops.

**Required in `vite.config.ts`:**
```ts
server: {
  watch: {
    ignored: [
      '**/test-vault/**',
      '**/.cortex/**', 
      '**/.claude/**',
      '**/vault/**',
      '**/node_modules/**',
      '**/.git/**'
    ],
  }
}
```

**Plus content-equality guard in writers:** Check if existing file content matches before writing.

---

## Phase B (Plan Execution) Rules

### Auth Strategy

Phase B spawns **Max-plan Claude Code sessions** via `--setting-sources user`, NOT Vertex AI.

**Correct spawn command:**
```bash
claude --print --verbose \
  --setting-sources user \
  --strict-mcp-config --mcp-config <path> \
  # ... other flags
```

**Why:** User has Max plan (unlimited). `--setting-sources user` loads user-level settings (`~/.claude/settings.json` with OAuth) but skips project-level hooks (`<vault>/.claude/settings.json`) to avoid recursion.

**Do NOT use:**
- `--bare` — kills OAuth/Max plan auth
- Vertex env translation — unnecessary for spawned claude processes

### DO NOT Use --include-hook-events Flag

This flag causes `claude --print --output-format stream-json` to exit immediately with 0 events under Tauri shell spawn.

**Why:** Unknown root cause. Flag validates fine but breaks every spawn. Fixed in commit `69cacb0`.

**Where to check:** `src-tauri/src/run/execute.rs::build_claude_args`

---

## Cortex's Three LLM Contexts (Do Not Conflate)

1. **Interactive Claude Code (Max plan OAuth)** — user runs directly, calls Cortex MCP server
2. **`cortex-extract` background job (Vertex AI)** — standalone KG extraction after session ends
3. **Phase B spawned Claude Code (Max plan OAuth)** — orchestrator launches plan execution

### Vertex AI Auth (cortex-extract ONLY)

**Env vars (load from `~/Desktop/Cortex/.env` via `dotenvy` at startup):**
- `GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON` or `GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON`
- `GCP_PROJECT_ID` or `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT_ID` (verified: `admachina-atomic-test-84`)
- `GCP_REGION` (default: `global`, verified: `global` and `us-east5`)

**Endpoint:**
```
POST https://{region}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{region}/publishers/anthropic/models/{model_id}:rawPredict
```
(If `region == "global"`, drop the `{region}-` prefix)

**Body:** Standard Anthropic Messages API, but:
- **Omit** `model` field (it's in URL)
- **Add** `"anthropic_version": "vertex-2023-10-16"`

**Auth:** `gcp_auth::AuthenticationManager::get_token()`, pass as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

**Model IDs (verified working 2026-04-09):**
- `claude-haiku-4-5@20251001` — fast, use for cortex-extract
- `claude-sonnet-4-5@20250929` — balanced
- `claude-opus-4-6` — powerful

**Structured output:** Vertex Anthropic does NOT support native structured output. Use forced `tool_use` with `tool_choice: {type: "tool", name: "..."}`.

**Do NOT:**
- Shell out to `claude --bare -p` for Cortex LLM calls
- Call `api.anthropic.com` — user's Anthropic account has zero credit balance

**Reference implementation:** `~/Desktop/future-stuff/future-labs/lib/agent/provider/registry.ts`

---

## UI Design Rules

### DO NOT:
- Use Playwright screenshot loops to evaluate design
- Tweak padding values and call it "design"
- Make incremental changes without understanding what's structurally wrong

### DO:
- Go component-by-component: read, understand, rewrite properly
- Study real apps (Obsidian, Linear, VS Code) before writing CSS
- Add proper depth (shadows, layering, visual hierarchy)
- Ensure text never touches container edges (proper inner padding)

**Recurring issues to fix:**
- Text crammed against edges (headers, buttons)
- No visual depth or hierarchy
- Section titles wrong size
- Sidebar header text at left edge
- Buttons with no inner margin

---

## test-vault/ Notes

**DO use:** `test-vault/patterns/` — curated docs (Safari 15 Compatibility, Parallel Agent Teams, Self-Bootstrapping)  
**DO NOT use:** `test-vault/sessions/` — auto-captured during dev, messy/incomplete

---

## Development Workflow

**Build:** `bun run tauri dev` (full stack), `bun run dev` (frontend only)  
**Gate checks:** `cargo check --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml && bun run build`

---

## MCP Server

Cortex runs its own MCP server for knowledge graph operations. Tools include:
- `cortex/search` — semantic search
- `cortex/get-note` — retrieve note by path
- `cortex/entity-profile` — get entity from KG
- `cortex/query-graph` — graph queries
- And more...

Phase B-spawned Claude processes get a restricted MCP config via `--strict-mcp-config` to control which servers they can access.

---

### Frontend Components
- Sidebar panels: `src/components/sidebar/`
- Main views: `src/components/`

### Backend Commands
- Vault ops: `src-tauri/src/commands/vault.rs`

### Configuration

- Vite config: `vite.config.ts`
- Environment: `~/Desktop/Cortex/.env` (loaded via `dotenvy`)

### Documentation
- This file: `CLAUDE.md`
- Project patterns: `test-vault/patterns/Pattern - *.md`
- Auto-captured sessions: `test-vault/sessions/` (noisy, use with caution)

---

## When In Doubt

1. Check existing implementations in the same category
2. Read the relevant `test-vault/patterns/` note if one exists  
3. Verify Safari 15 compat before writing frontend code

15	## CRITICAL: Safari 15 Compatibility (macOS 12 WKWebView)
16	
17	Tauri on macOS 12 uses Safari 15's WKWebView. Modern CSS features cause **blank pages**.
18	
19	### DO NOT USE in any frontend code:
20	- `oklch()` colors — NOT supported
21	- `color-mix()` — NOT supported  
22	- **Tailwind v4 color utility classes** (`bg-red-500`, `text-blue-400`, etc.) — emit `oklch()` internally
23	- Regex lookbehind `(?<=...)` or `(?<!...)` — NOT supported
24	
25	### DO USE:
26	- Inline `style={{ padding: 16, marginBottom: 20 }}` for spacing
27	- CSS variables with **hex values only**: `var(--accent)` where `--accent: #3b82f6`
28	- Lucide icons (pure SVG, always safe)
29	- Tailwind layout utilities: `flex`, `flex-col`, `min-w-0`, `overflow-*`, `gap-*` (anything non-color)
30	- Hex colors for specific shades: `color: "#ef4444"` NOT `color: "red-500"`
31

33

36	- `src/components/sidebar/PlansPanel.tsx`

40

46	
47	macOS Finder/Dock launches don't inherit shell PATH. `/opt/homebrew/bin`, `/usr/local/bin`, `$HOME/.local/bin` are missing.
48	
49	**Fix (required for all spawns):**
50	```rust
51	let current_path = std::env::var("PATH").unwrap_or_default();
52	let extended_path = format!("/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:{}", current_path);
53	shell.command("claude")
54	    .env("PATH", &extended_path)  // Always extend PATH
55	    .spawn()?;
56	```
57	
58	**Reference:** `src-tauri/src/run/execute.rs`
59	
60	### 2. Shell Spawn Requires TWO Permission Types
61	
62	`tauri-plugin-shell` 2.x needs BOTH:
63	- `shell:allow-execute` — for `.execute()` 
64	- `shell:allow-spawn` — for `.spawn()`
65	
66	Plus `shell:allow-kill` and `shell:allow-stdin-write` as needed.
67	
68	**Reference:** `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`
69	
70	### 3. window.prompt/alert/confirm Don't Work
71	
72	WKWebView blocks legacy browser dialogs. They return null or no-op.
73	
74	**Use instead:** React state + inline input fields with `autoFocus`, Enter-to-submit, Escape-to-cancel.
75	
76	**Reference:** `src/components/sidebar/PlansPanel.tsx` (+ New Plan and ✨ Draft Plan buttons)
77	
78	### 4. Vite HMR Must Ignore Vault Paths
79	
80	Backend writes to `.claude/settings.json`, `.cortex/kg.json` trigger infinite reload loops.
81	
82	**Required in `vite.config.ts`:**
83	```ts

85	  watch: {
86	    ignored: [
87	      '**/test-vault/**',
88	      '**/.cortex/**', 
89	      '**/.claude/**',
90	      '**/vault/**',
91	      '**/node_modules/**',
92	      '**/.git/**'
93	    ],
94	  }
95	}
96	```
97	
98	**Plus content-equality guard in writers:** Check if existing file content matches before writing.
99	
100	---
101	
102	## Phase B (Plan Execution) Rules
103	
104	### Auth Strategy
105	
106	Phase B spawns **Max-plan Claude Code sessions** via `--setting-sources user`, NOT Vertex AI.
107	
108	**Correct spawn command:**
109	```bash
110	claude --print --verbose \
111	  --setting-sources user \
112	  --strict-mcp-config --mcp-config <path> \
113	  # ... other flags
114	```
115	
116	**Why:** User has Max plan (unlimited). `--setting-sources user` loads user-level settings (`~/.claude/settings.json` with OAuth) but skips project-level hooks (`<vault>/.claude/settings.json`) to avoid recursion.
117	
118	**Do NOT use:**
119	- `--bare` — kills OAuth/Max plan auth
120	- Vertex env translation — unnecessary for spawned claude processes
121	
122	### DO NOT Use --include-hook-events Flag
123	
124	This flag causes `claude --print --output-format stream-json` to exit immediately with 0 events under Tauri shell spawn.
125	
126	**Why:** Unknown root cause. Flag validates fine but breaks every spawn. Fixed in commit `69cacb0`.
127	
128	**Where to check:** `src-tauri/src/run/execute.rs::build_claude_args`

135	2. **`cortex-extract` background job (Vertex AI)** — standalone KG extraction after session ends

# Cortex Project Instructions

Cortex is a Tauri 2.x desktop app (Rust + React + TypeScript) that provides knowledge graph-powered note-taking with AI-assisted planning. This file encodes hard-won constraints that future Claude Code sessions must follow.

## Project Structure

- `src-tauri/` — Rust backend (Tauri commands, MCP server, KG extraction)
- `src/components/` — React frontend components
- `cortex-extract/` — Standalone KG extraction binary (Vertex AI)
- `test-vault/` — Test notes (patterns/ = docs, sessions/ = auto-generated)
- Key files: `vite.config.ts`, `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`

---

## CRITICAL: Safari 15 Compatibility (macOS 12 WKWebView)

Tauri on macOS 12 uses Safari 15's WKWebView. Modern CSS features cause **blank pages**.

### DO NOT USE in any frontend code:
- `oklch()` colors — NOT supported
- `color-mix()` — NOT supported  
- **Tailwind v4 color utility classes** (`bg-red-500`, `text-blue-400`, etc.) — emit `oklch()` internally
- Regex lookbehind `(?<=...)` or `(?<!...)` — NOT supported

### DO USE:
- Inline `style={{ padding: 16, marginBottom: 20 }}` for spacing
- CSS variables with **hex values only**: `var(--accent)` where `--accent: #3b82f6`
- Lucide icons (pure SVG, always safe)
- Tailwind layout utilities: `flex`, `flex-col`, `min-w-0`, `overflow-*`, `gap-*` (anything non-color)
- Hex colors for specific shades: `color: "#ef4444"` NOT `color: "red-500"`

**Verify:** `grep -rn oklch src/` must stay empty.

**Reference implementations:**
- `src/components/sidebar/SessionsPanel.tsx`
- `src/components/sidebar/PlansPanel.tsx`
- `src/components/sidebar/LiveSessionView.tsx`

**Why:** Discovered during UI polish — whole pages went blank. Documented in `test-vault/patterns/Pattern - Safari 15 Compatibility.md`.

---

## Tauri v2 Gotchas

### 1. GUI-Launched Apps Have Barebones PATH

macOS Finder/Dock launches don't inherit shell PATH. `/opt/homebrew/bin`, `/usr/local/bin`, `$HOME/.local/bin` are missing.

**Fix (required for all spawns):**
```rust
let current_path = std::env::var("PATH").unwrap_or_default();
let extended_path = format!("/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:{}", current_path);
shell.command("claude")
    .env("PATH", &extended_path)  // Always extend PATH
    .spawn()?;
```

**Reference:** `src-tauri/src/run/execute.rs`

### 2. Shell Spawn Requires TWO Permission Types

`tauri-plugin-shell` 2.x needs BOTH:
- `shell:allow-execute` — for `.execute()` 
- `shell:allow-spawn` — for `.spawn()`

Plus `shell:allow-kill` and `shell:allow-stdin-write` as needed.

**Reference:** `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`

### 3. window.prompt/alert/confirm Don't Work

WKWebView blocks legacy browser dialogs. They return null or no-op.

**Use instead:** React state + inline input fields with `autoFocus`, Enter-to-submit, Escape-to-cancel.

**Reference:** `src/components/sidebar/PlansPanel.tsx` (+ New Plan and ✨ Draft Plan buttons)

### 4. Vite HMR Must Ignore Vault Paths

Backend writes to `.claude/settings.json`, `.cortex/kg.json` trigger infinite reload loops.

**Required in `vite.config.ts`:**
```ts
server: {
  watch: {
    ignored: [
      '**/test-vault/**',
      '**/.cortex/**', 
      '**/.claude/**',
      '**/vault/**',
      '**/node_modules/**',
      '**/.git/**'
    ],
  }
}
```

**Plus content-equality guard in writers:** Check if existing file content matches before writing.

---

## Phase B (Plan Execution) Rules

### Auth Strategy

Phase B spawns **Max-plan Claude Code sessions** via `--setting-sources user`, NOT Vertex AI.

**Correct spawn command:**
```bash
claude --print --verbose \
  --setting-sources user \
  --strict-mcp-config --mcp-config <path> \
  # ... other flags
```

**Why:** User has Max plan (unlimited). `--setting-sources user` loads user-level settings (`~/.claude/settings.json` with OAuth) but skips project-level hooks (`<vault>/.claude/settings.json`) to avoid recursion.

**Do NOT use:**
- `--bare` — kills OAuth/Max plan auth
- Vertex env translation — unnecessary for spawned claude processes

### DO NOT Use --include-hook-events Flag

This flag causes `claude --print --output-format stream-json` to exit immediately with 0 events under Tauri shell spawn.

**Why:** Unknown root cause. Flag validates fine but breaks every spawn. Fixed in commit `69cacb0`.

**Where to check:** `src-tauri/src/run/execute.rs::build_claude_args`

---

## Cortex's Three LLM Contexts (Do Not Conflate)

1. **Interactive Claude Code (Max plan OAuth)** — user runs directly, calls Cortex MCP server
2. **`cortex-extract` background job (Vertex AI)** — standalone KG extraction after session ends
3. **Phase B spawned Claude Code (Max plan OAuth)** — orchestrator launches plan execution

### Vertex AI Auth (cortex-extract ONLY)

**Env vars (load from `~/Desktop/Cortex/.env` via `dotenvy` at startup):**
- `GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON` or `GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON`
- `GCP_PROJECT_ID` or `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT_ID` (verified: `admachina-atomic-test-84`)
- `GCP_REGION` (default: `global`, verified: `global` and `us-east5`)

**Endpoint:**
```
POST https://{region}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{region}/publishers/anthropic/models/{model_id}:rawPredict
```
(If `region == "global"`, drop the `{region}-` prefix)

**Body:** Standard Anthropic Messages API, but:
- **Omit** `model` field (it's in URL)
- **Add** `"anthropic_version": "vertex-2023-10-16"`

**Auth:** `gcp_auth::AuthenticationManager::get_token()`, pass as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

**Model IDs (verified working 2026-04-09):**
- `claude-haiku-4-5@20251001` — fast, use for cortex-extract
- `claude-sonnet-4-5@20250929` — balanced
- `claude-opus-4-6` — powerful

**Structured output:** Vertex Anthropic does NOT support native structured output. Use forced `tool_use` with `tool_choice: {type: "tool", name: "..."}`.

**Do NOT:**
- Shell out to `claude --bare -p` for Cortex LLM calls
- Call `api.anthropic.com` — user's Anthropic account has zero credit balance

**Reference implementation:** `~/Desktop/future-stuff/future-labs/lib/agent/provider/registry.ts`

---

## UI Design Rules

**DO:** Study real apps (Obsidian, Linear) before writing CSS. Go component-by-component. Add depth (shadows, hierarchy). Ensure text never touches edges.  
**DON'T:** Playwright screenshot loops, incremental padding tweaks without understanding structural issues.  
**Fix:** Text crammed at edges, no visual depth, wrong section title sizes, buttons with no inner margin.

---

## test-vault/ Notes

**DO use:** `test-vault/patterns/` — curated docs (Safari 15 Compatibility, Parallel Agent Teams, Self-Bootstrapping)  
**DO NOT use:** `test-vault/sessions/` — auto-captured during dev, messy/incomplete

---

## Development Workflow

**Build:** `bun run tauri dev` (full stack), `bun run dev` (frontend only)  
**Gate checks:** `cargo check --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml && bun run build`

---

## MCP Server

Cortex runs its own MCP server with tools: `cortex/search`, `cortex/get-note`, `cortex/entity-profile`, `cortex/query-graph`, etc. Phase B spawns get restricted MCP via `--strict-mcp-config`.

---

## File Paths Quick Reference

### Frontend Components
- Sidebar panels: `src/components/sidebar/`
- Main views: `src/components/`

### Backend Commands
- Vault ops: `src-tauri/src/commands/vault.rs`
- Plan execution: `src-tauri/src/run/execute.rs`
- Plan commands: `src-tauri/src/commands/plans.rs`
- Session capture: `src-tauri/src/commands/sessions.rs`

### Configuration
- Tauri permissions: `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`
- Vite config: `vite.config.ts`
- Environment: `~/Desktop/Cortex/.env` (loaded via `dotenvy`)

### Documentation
- This file: `CLAUDE.md`
- Project patterns: `test-vault/patterns/Pattern - *.md`
- Auto-captured sessions: `test-vault/sessions/` (noisy, use with caution)

---

## When In Doubt

1. Check existing implementations in the same category
2. Read the relevant `test-vault/patterns/` note if one exists  
3. Verify Safari 15 compat before writing frontend code
4. Run `cargo check` + `bun run build` before claiming "it works"
5. Never bypass Tauri security (don't add blanket shell permissions)
